sections.title: 1. பாடத்தின் முழுமையான சுருக்கம் (Detailed Summary)
sections.content:
இந்தக் கதை (The Last Stone Carver) ஒரு முதிய சிற்பி, அவருடைய மகன் கோபால், மற்றும் அவருடைய வேலைக்காரச் சிறுவன் சலீம் ஆகியோருக்கு இடையிலான உணர்வுப்பூர்வமான உறவைப் பற்றியது.

Section I: முதிய சிற்பியின் மகன் கோபால், பல தலைமுறைகளாகச் செய்து வரும் சிலை செதுக்கும் தொழிலை விட்டுவிட்டு ஆக்ராவிற்குச் (Agra) செல்ல முடிவு செய்கிறான். குறைவான வருமானமே கிடைப்பதால், மற்றவர்களைப் போலப் பெருமளவில் ஆஷ்ட்ரே (ashtrays), மெழுகுவர்த்தித் தாங்கி (candlestands) ஆகியவற்றைத் தயாரித்துப் பணம் சம்பாதிக்க விரும்புகிறான். முதியவர், இது வெறும் வேலை அல்ல, கடவுளுக்குச் செய்யும் சேவை (service to God) என்று விளக்கியும் கோபால் கேட்காமல் சென்றுவிடுகிறான். முதியவர், தான் மட்டுமே இங்கு எஞ்சியிருக்கும் கடைசிச் சிற்பி என்றும், தனக்கு உதவியாக இருக்கும் அனாதைச் சிறுவன் சலீமும் (Salim) ஒரு நாள் தன்னை விட்டுச் சென்றுவிடுவான் என்றும் எண்ணி வருந்துகிறார். எனினும், தான் செதுக்கிக் கொண்டிருக்கும் கிருஷ்ணர் சிலையை எப்படியாவது முடித்துவிட வேண்டும் என உறுதிகொள்கிறார்.

Section II: கோபால் சென்று ஒரு மாதம் ஆகிவிட்டது. முதியவர் சோர்வின்றி வேலை செய்கிறார். அவர் செதுக்கும் கிருஷ்ணர் சிலை (Statue of Krishna) மிகச் சிறப்பாக வந்து கொண்டிருக்கிறது. ஆனால் அவருக்கு வயதாகிவிட்டதாலும், சரியாக உணவு உண்ணாததாலும் உடல் சோர்வடைகிறது. சிலைக்குரிய முகம் மற்றும் கைகளின் நுணுக்கங்களை (finer details) எவ்வாறு செதுக்குவது என்ற கவலை அவருக்கு ஏற்படுகிறது. சிறுவன் சலீம், சிறந்த கலை என்பது மனதின் ஆழத்திலிருந்து (from deep inside the heart) வரவேண்டும் என்று அவருக்கு நினைவூட்டுகிறான். ஒரு நாள் இரவு, உடல் மிகவும் சோர்வடைந்து முதியவர் மயங்கி விழுகிறார். விழித்துப் பார்க்கையில் அவர் தனது படுக்கையில் இருப்பதை உணர்கிறார்.

Section III: மறுநாள் காலையில், முதியவர் தனது பட்டறையிலிருந்து உளி மற்றும் சுத்தியலின் சத்தம் கேட்பதை உணர்கிறார். கோபால் திரும்பி வந்துவிட்டான் என்று எண்ணி மகிழ்ச்சியுடன் பட்டறைக்குச் செல்கிறார். ஆனால் அங்கே கிருஷ்ணரின் முகத்தை மிக நேர்த்தியாகச் செதுக்கிக் கொண்டிருப்பது கோபால் அல்ல; அது சலீம்! அதிர்ச்சியும் மகிழ்ச்சியும் அடைந்த முதியவர் சலீமைத் தட்டிக் கொடுக்கிறார். கடந்த இரண்டு ஆண்டுகளாக குவாரியில் (quarry) தான் ரகசியமாகச் சிலைகள் வடிக்கப் பழகியதாக சலீம் கூறுகிறான். மேலும், தன்னை ஒரு சிற்பியாக ஏற்றுக்கொள்ளும்படி முதியவரிடம் வேண்டுகிறான். முதியவர் அவனைக் கட்டியணைத்து, 'உனக்கு நான்கற்றுத் தர எதுவுமில்லை; சிறந்த சிற்பிக்குரிய திறமை உன் கையிலும் மனதிலும் உள்ளது. நீ நாட்டின் சிறந்த சிற்பிகளில் ஒருவனாக வருவாய்' என்று மனமார வாழ்த்துகிறார்.

sections.title: 2. கூடுதல் பகுதிகள் மற்றும் குறிப்புகள் (Extra Points & Character Sketch)
sections.content:
- The Old Man (Masterjee): பல தலைமுறைகளாகச் சிற்பக் கலையைப் பின்பற்றி வரும் முதியவர். பணத்தை விடக் கலைக்கும், கடவுளின் சேவைக்கும் அதிக மதிப்பளிக்கிறார்.
- Gopal (கோபால்): முதியவரின் மகன். கலையின் புனிதத்தை விடப் பணமே முக்கியம் என நினைத்து ஆக்ராவிற்குச் சென்றவன்.
- Salim (சலீம்): அனாதைச் சிறுவன். 5 வருடங்களுக்கு முன் மழையில் நனைந்து அடைக்கலம் தேடி வந்து, பின் வேலைக்காரனாக மாறியவன். ஆனால், கலை மீதுள்ள ஆர்வத்தால் ரகசியமாக உழைத்துச் சிறந்த சிற்பியாக உருவெடுக்கிறான்.
- The Masterpiece: முதியவர் வடிக்கும் சிலை - புல்லாங்குழல் ஊதும் கிருஷ்ணர் சிலை (Krishna holding the flute delicately to his lips).
- The Author: இக்கதையை எழுதியவர் சிக்ருன் ஸ்ரீவஸ்தவ் (Sigrun Srivastav). இவர் ஜெர்மனியைப் பூர்வீகமாகக் கொண்ட ஒரு இந்திய எழுத்தாளர், சிற்பி மற்றும் ஓவியர். குழந்தைகளுக்காக 25க்கும் மேற்பட்ட புத்தகங்களை எழுதியுள்ளார்.

sections.title: 3. நினைவில் கொள்க மற்றும் முக்கிய குறிப்புகள் (Points to Remember)
sections.content:
- கோபால் எதற்காகத் தொழிலை விட்டுச் சென்றான்? : Working for a pittance (குறைந்த வருமானம்) மற்றும் ஆக்ராவில் mass produce செய்து பணம் சம்பாதிக்க.
- சலீம் யாரிடம் வேலை செய்தான்? : The old master craftsman (Masterjee).
- முதியவர் எதனால் மயங்கி விழுந்தார்? : Weakness, lack of food, and overworking late into the night.
- சலீம் எங்கு ரகசியமாகச் சிலை செதுக்கப் பழகினான்? : In the quarry (கல் குவாரி).
- சலீம் என்ன வேலை செய்து கொண்டிருந்தான்? : He was working on the face of the statue, on the eyebrows, arching over a pair of fine eyes.
- கலை எங்கிருந்து வர வேண்டும்? : Deep inside the heart (மனதின் ஆழத்திலிருந்து).

sections.title: 4. அருஞ்சொற்பொருள் (Glossary)
sections.content:
1. congested - overcrowded (நெரிசலான)
2. grunted - made a low sound (முனகுதல் / அதிருப்தியை வெளிப்படுத்துதல்)
3. crouching - sitting on heels (குந்தியிருத்தல்)
4. determination - firmness to do something (உறுதி)
5. serene - calm (அமைதியான)
6. blurred - became unclear (மங்கலான)
7. blush - show shyness (வெட்கப்படுதல்)
8. stumbled - lost balance (தடுமாறுதல்)
9. immobilized - stopped from moving (அசையாமல் நிற்றல் / உறைந்து போதல்)
10. tottered over - moved unsteadily way (தடுமாறி நடத்தல்)
11. startled - surprised (திடுக்கிடுதல் / ஆச்சரியப்படுதல்)

sections.title: 5. புத்தகப் பின்பக்க வினாக்கள் - ஒரு மதிப்பெண் (Book Back Questions)
sections.content:
A. Meaning of sentences (வாக்கியத்தின் பொருள்):
1. The white washed walls were stained by many monsoons. -> (a) The walls were made dirty by rains.
2. I am tired of working for a pittance. -> (b) He didn’t want to work as he gets low income for his work.
3. His face was ashen. -> (a) He looked pale and dull.
4. He is drenched to the skin. -> (b) He is thoroughly wet.

B. Read the sentences and number them in correct order (வரிசைப்படுத்துக):
1. The old man worked tirelessly on the sculpture. (4)
2. Over days, he felt very weak. (7)
3. He had a strong wish to finish it in time. (6)
4. He thought he wouldn’t be able to finish it. (3)
5. He wished to have Gopal with him. (1)
6. He realized that Gopal must learn to carve the finer details. (5)
7. Salim felt that it should come from within. (2)
(Correct Order: 4, 7, 6, 3, 1, 5, 2)

C. Read Section III and answer:
Para 1: 1. Who listened to the chipping sound? -> The old man. 2. Who was working with the hammer and chisel? -> Salim.
Para 4: 1. Who was staring? -> The old man. 2. Who was the young stone carver? -> Salim. 3. What was he working on? -> The face of the statue (eyebrows/eyes).

D. Match the tools with art forms (கருவிகள் - கலை):
1. Hammer and chisel -> Sculpture
2. Paint brush and palette -> Painting
3. Moulds and roll pin -> Pottery
4. Scissors and paper -> Collage (or Origami)
5. Needle and thread -> Embroidery

E. Grammar - Passive Voice Fill in the blanks:
1. Paper is produced (produce) from wood.
2. The fibres of wood from trees are mixed (mix) with water...
3. Later, it is dried (dry) to make paper.
4. This method was invented (invent) in 2nd BCE in China.
5. Papyrus plants were used (use) by Egyptians...

quiz.question: Who flung the hammer and the chisel to the ground at the beginning of the story?
quiz.explanation: The story begins with the young man (Gopal) flinging his hammer and chisel, declaring he is leaving the work.

quiz.question: Why was the young man (Gopal) leaving his father and the work?
options: He wanted to become an actor.
He was tired of working for a pittance (very little money).
He wanted to join the army.
quiz.explanation: He states, 'I’m tired of working for a pittance for the committee...'

quiz.question: How did the old man view his work as a stone carver?
options: Just a way to make a lot of money.
quiz.explanation: The old man says, 'It’s not only the money that matters, son. It’s the service, our service to God.'

quiz.question: Where were all the other stone carvers going to mass-produce cheap items?
quiz.explanation: Gopal mentions, 'You have to mass produce, mass produce like all the others in Agra.'

quiz.question: What kind of cheap items were they mass-producing in Agra?
quiz.explanation: The old man laments that in Agra they are 'turning out cheap candlestands, paperweights and ashtrays by the dozen.'

quiz.question: Who was Salim?
quiz.explanation: Salim is described as 'the servant boy' who brings tea for the Masterjee.

quiz.question: What was the old man sculpting after his son left?
quiz.explanation: The text mentions he was working on 'the strong, straight shoulders of Krishna... holding the flute'.

quiz.question: How did Salim come to work for the old man?
options: He was hired from an agency.
He was an orphan boy who begged for shelter during a monsoon storm.
He paid the old man to teach him.
quiz.explanation: The text says, 'The old man looked at the orphan boy... Drenched to the skin... he had begged for shelter during monsoon storm.'

quiz.question: What does the word 'pittance' mean in the context of the story?
quiz.explanation: Working for a pittance means working for very little money.

quiz.question: What does 'ashen' mean?
quiz.explanation: In the book back exercise, 'His face was ashen' means 'He looked pale and dull'.

quiz.question: How many years had Salim been working for the old man?
quiz.explanation: The text says: 'The old man looked at the orphan boy who had come to work for him five years ago.'

quiz.question: What was the old man's physical condition as days went by?
options: His strength ebbed, shoulders ached, and vision blurred.
He gained a lot of weight.
quiz.explanation: ...the old man felt his strength ebb. His shoulders began to ache, his arms felt heavy and his vision blurred.

quiz.question: What part of the statue was the old man struggling with because it required 'finer details'?
quiz.explanation: The old man felt, 'It was the features and hands that gave him trouble. There was something missing...'

quiz.question: According to Salim, where does true art or the 'finer details' come from?
options: From somewhere deep inside you (the heart)
quiz.explanation: Salim whispered, 'Because it comes from somewhere deep inside you... From deep inside here!' and he pointed to his heart.

quiz.question: What happened to the old man in the early hours of the morning while working?
options: His son Gopal returned to help him.
He collapsed and sank into darkness (fainted).
quiz.explanation: His old body sagged... His forehead struck Krishna's flute... 'Hai Ram,' he muttered, and sank into a comfortable darkness.

quiz.question: When the old man woke up, what sound reached his ears from the workshop?
options: The chipping sound of the chisel
quiz.explanation: From the workshop, the chipping sound of the chisel reached his ears.

quiz.question: Who did the old man initially think had returned to finish the statue?
quiz.explanation: He thought: 'Gopal! He was back! Gopal had returned.'

quiz.question: Who was actually working on the face of the statue?
quiz.explanation: But it wasn’t his son... It was Salim, his servant.

quiz.question: Where had Salim secretly practiced stone carving for almost two years?
quiz.explanation: Salim said, 'I have practised secretly for almost two years in the quarry.'

quiz.question: What was the old man's reaction when he saw Salim carving?
options: He was furious and beat him.
He chased him out of the workshop.
First shock, then a feeling of great relief and happiness.
quiz.explanation: The first wave of shock, fear and anger passed to give way to a feeling of great relief and happiness.

quiz.question: What did Salim want the old man to do for him?
options: To teach him to be a stone carver.
quiz.explanation: Salim asked, 'I want to become a stonecarver. Will you please teach me, Masterjee?'

quiz.question: What did the old man tell Salim at the end of the story?
options: You have to practice for ten more years.
There's nothing I can teach you; you have it in your hands and heart.
quiz.explanation: He said, 'There’s nothing I can teach you my son beta. Go ahead. You have it in your hands and in your heart.'

quiz.question: Who is the author of the story 'The Last Stone Carver'?
quiz.explanation: The author mentioned at the end of the text is Sigrun Srivastav.

quiz.question: What does the word 'congested' mean?
quiz.explanation: The glossary defines 'congested' as overcrowded.

quiz.question: What does 'crouching' mean?
quiz.explanation: The glossary defines 'crouching' as sitting on heels.

quiz.question: Match the tool with the art form: Hammer and chisel
quiz.explanation: A hammer and chisel are the primary tools used for creating a sculpture.

quiz.question: Match the tool with the art form: Paint brush and palette
quiz.explanation: Paint brush and palette are used in Painting.

quiz.question: In the passive voice, what receives the action?
quiz.explanation: According to the Language Check Point: 'In the passive voice, the subject receives the action.'

quiz.question: Identify the correct passive voice for: 'The dog chews the shoe.'
options: The shoe was chewed by the dog.
The dog is chewing the shoe.
The shoe is chewed by the dog.
The dog has chewed the shoe.
quiz.explanation: Present tense active (chews) becomes present tense passive (is chewed).

quiz.question: Identify the correct passive voice for: 'My mom has made a cake.'
options: A cake is made by my mom.
A cake was made by my mom.
A cake has been made by my mom.
A cake had been made by my mom.
quiz.explanation: Present perfect active (has made) becomes present perfect passive (has been made).

quiz.question: What does 'serene' mean?
quiz.explanation: The glossary defines 'serene' as calm. The old man sculpted Krishna's 'serene face'.

quiz.question: Fill in the blank with suitable passive form: 'Paper _______(produce) from wood.'
quiz.explanation: It is a general fact, so simple present passive 'is produced' is correct.

quiz.question: Fill in the blank with suitable passive form: 'This method ________(invent) in 2nd BCE in China.'
quiz.explanation: It refers to a past event (2nd BCE), so simple past passive 'was invented' is used.

quiz.question: What does 'tottered over' mean?
quiz.explanation: The glossary defines 'tottered over' as moved unsteadily way (due to weakness or shock).

quiz.question: Why did Gopal say the stone carver's work has changed?
options: Because you can't live on sculptures for temples only; you have to mass produce.
quiz.explanation: Gopal states, 'You can’t live on sculptures for temples only. You have to mass produce...'

quiz.question:
Assertion (A): The old man was happy when Gopal left for Agra.
Reason (R): The old man wanted to work alone on the Krishna statue.
options: Both A and R are true.
Both A and R are false.
A is true, but R is false.
A is false, but R is true.
quiz.explanation: The old man was sad and disappointed when Gopal left, and he didn't want to work alone; he wished Gopal had stayed to learn.

quiz.question: What did Salim offer the old man when he found him tired in the workshop?
quiz.explanation: He held out a glass of steaming tea in his hand.

quiz.question: How long had the tradition of stone carving been kept up in the old man's family?
options: He was the first stone carver in his family
quiz.explanation: The old man says, 'We have kept up this tradition for hundreds of years...'

quiz.question: Analogy: Gopal : Mass production :: Old Man : ________
quiz.explanation: Gopal values mass production and money, while the Old Man values tradition and service to God.

quiz.question: What does the phrase 'drenched to the skin' mean?
quiz.explanation: As per the book back exercise, 'drenched to the skin' means 'thoroughly wet'.

quiz.question: Why did the old man's son call him a fool (implied by asking him to 'Wake up')?
options: Because he was sleeping during work.
Because he didn't understand that times have changed and money is important.
quiz.explanation: Gopal says 'Times have changed... Come on, Father! Wake up', implying the old man is foolishly clinging to old ways without making money.

quiz.question: What gave the old man happiness and confidence despite his son leaving?
options: The cool metal of the tools (chisel and hammer).
The thought of moving to Agra.
quiz.explanation: The text says: 'The cool metal of the tools filled him with happiness and confidence. He loved his work...'

quiz.question: What was the old man's biggest piece of work and also his last?
quiz.explanation: The statue of Krishna with a flute was his biggest piece of work and his last.

quiz.question: What made the old man realize that he couldn't finish the statue?
options: His strength ebbed, vision blurred, and his arms felt heavy.
quiz.explanation: Due to old age and weakness, his strength ebbed, making him feel he wouldn't be able to finish it.

quiz.question: According to the old man, what was missing in Gopal's figures?
options: Something that can't be taught (the finer details)
quiz.explanation: The old man felt, 'There was something missing in his figures. That something which can’t be taught.'

quiz.question: What did Salim advise the old man to do when he felt weak?
options: To eat rice, vegetables, and curd.
To ask Gopal to come back.
quiz.explanation: Salim said, 'Please have some rice and vegetables... Have the curd.'

quiz.question: What does 'immobilized' mean?
quiz.explanation: The glossary defines 'immobilized' as stopped from moving (usually due to shock or fear).

quiz.question: Who muttered 'Hai Ram' and sank into darkness?
quiz.explanation: When the old man fainted from exhaustion, he muttered 'Hai Ram'.

quiz.question: What is the author Sigrun Srivastav's origin?
quiz.explanation: The author box states: 'Sigrun Srivastav is an Indian author of German origin.'

quiz.question: Situation: A student paints a picture mechanically to win a prize, while another paints with deep emotion expressing his soul. According to Salim, which student is acting like a true artist?
quiz.explanation: Salim emphasizes that true art comes 'from somewhere deep inside you'.

quiz.question:
Assertion (A): The old man was disappointed when he saw Salim carving the statue.
Reason (R): Salim was ruining the face of the Krishna statue.
options: Both A and R are true.
Both A and R are false.
A is true, but R is false.
A is false, but R is true.
quiz.explanation: The old man felt great relief and happiness (not disappointment) because Salim was carving the face beautifully with fine details.

quiz.question: What was the final result of Salim's secret practice?
options: He became a skilled stone carver capable of doing finer details.
He forgot how to serve tea.
He was forced to go to Agra.
quiz.explanation: He learned the art so well that he could carve the difficult features of Krishna's face, proving he was a skilled carver.

quiz.question: Which tool is paired with 'Needle and thread' in the vocabulary section?
quiz.explanation: Needle and thread are used for Embroidery.

quiz.question: What is the difference between 'look' and 'watch' according to the text?
options: Look is natural; watch is with effort.
Look is making an effort to see; watch is looking for a period of time.
Look is for moving objects; watch is for still objects.
quiz.explanation: The box says: look - make an effort to see something; watch - something you look at for a period of time.

quiz.question: Change to passive voice: 'Junk food should be avoided.'
options: The sentence is already in passive voice.
quiz.explanation: 'Should be avoided' indicates the passive voice structure (modal + be + V3).

quiz.question: What did Gopal think of the old man's dedication to his work?
options: He thought it was foolish and didn't pay enough.
quiz.explanation: Gopal said, 'This kind of work just doesn’t pay!' and asked his father to 'Wake up'.

quiz.question: What did the old man mean when he thumped his chest and said, 'if you don’t have it here... then you’d better go to Agra'?
options: If you don't have money, go to Agra.
If you don't have true passion and feeling for art in your heart, you should just do commercial work.
If you don't have strong muscles, go to Agra.
If you don't have tools, go to Agra.
quiz.explanation: He meant that without inner devotion and feeling (the heart), one can only mass-produce items mechanically.

quiz.question: Which word is a synonym for 'startled'?
quiz.explanation: The glossary defines 'startled' as surprised.

quiz.question: What did Salim do when the old man approached him while he was carving?
options: He rose to his feet clumsily, hammer and chisel in hand.
He shouted at the old man.
quiz.explanation: The startled boy turned... He rose to his feet clumsily, the hammer and chisel still in his hands.

quiz.question: How did Salim feel about becoming a stone carver?
options: He thought it was an easy way to make money.
He fought the feeling for years but realized it was what he wanted to do most in life.
He hated it but did it to help the master.
He wanted to go to Agra to mass produce.
quiz.explanation: Salim said, 'For many years, I wanted to become a sculptor like you yet I fought the feeling. But it proved too strong.'

quiz.question: Analogy: Gopal : Left the master :: Salim : _________
quiz.explanation: While Gopal left his father for money, Salim stayed back and helped him finish the masterpiece.

quiz.question:
Assertion (A): The old man believed Salim would leave him one day.
Reason (R): Salim was also interested in mass-producing ashtrays in Agra.
options: Both A and R are true, and R is the correct explanation of A.
Both A and R are true, but R is not the correct explanation of A.
A is true, but R is false.
A is false, but R is true.
quiz.explanation: The old man knew Salim would leave him one day (maybe to find his own life), but Salim was NOT interested in mass-producing items; he wanted to be a true sculptor.

quiz.question: In the process of making soup, choose the correct passive form: 'The mixture ________ into a heavy-bottomed vessel.'
quiz.explanation: In describing a process, simple present passive is used: 'is poured'.

quiz.question: What is the ultimate message of 'The Last Stone Carver'?
options: Money is more important than tradition.
True art requires passion, devotion, and a connection from the heart, not just skill.
Stone carving is a dying art and should be abandoned.
Agra is the best place for sculptors.
quiz.explanation: The story highlights that while Gopal had skill, he lacked the 'heart' for it. Salim, despite being a servant, became a great artist because his art came from deep inside him.

quiz.question: Who muttered 'I...I...I...only wanted to help'?
quiz.explanation: When caught carving the statue, a scared Salim whispered, 'I...I...I...only wanted to help'.

quiz.question: What gave way to 'a feeling of great relief and happiness' in the old man?
options: The first wave of shock, fear and anger
His desire to go to Agra
The thought of his son Gopal
quiz.explanation: Upon seeing someone touching his masterpiece, he first felt shock, fear, and anger, which quickly turned to relief when he saw Salim doing a beautiful job.

quiz.question: What did the old man mean by calling Salim 'beta'?
options: He considered Salim as his own son.
quiz.explanation: By calling him 'beta' (son), the old man affectionately accepts Salim as his spiritual and artistic heir, replacing Gopal.

quiz.question: How did the old man bless Salim?
options: He wished him wealth in Agra.
He said he will be one of the country's finest stone carvers.
He said he will find his real parents.
He promised to pay him double wages.
quiz.explanation: The old man whispered, 'I know you will be one of the country’s finest stonecarvers.'

quiz.question: Which of the following is NOT true about the author Sigrun Srivastav?
quiz.explanation: The text explicitly states: 'As a writer she has written over 25 books for children of all ages.'

quiz.question: Identify the passive voice sentence from the following.
options: More than one lakh of rupees was stolen from the bank.
quiz.explanation: 'Was stolen' is the passive form. The other sentences are in active voice.
